message:
docs(tui/query): document Cx-first story for sync→async bridge (ft-xbnl0.2.2)

tui/query.rs has zero public async fn — it's a sync→async bridge where
the QueryClient trait methods are synchronous and own a dedicated
runtime_compat::Runtime that executes inner async blocks via
runtime.block_on(async { ... }). There is no public async surface to
thread &Cx through explicitly; Cx flows automatically inside each
block_on because runtime_compat::Runtime::block_on registers a root Cx
under the asupersync-runtime feature, and downstream helpers acquire
it via Cx::current().

Adds a module-level doc section that:
1. Names this file as visited and analyzed under ft-xbnl0.2.2
2. Explains the Cx-first story for the sync→async bridge shape
3. Records the trigger condition for adding a `_cx` variant later (if
   the TUI ever exposes an async fn query entry point)
4. Cross-references the nine files in the lane that did follow the
   standard _cx pattern

This closes tui/query.rs as "visited, no _cx variant needed" rather
than leaving it ambiguous.

Tenth file in the ft-xbnl0.2.2 Cx-first lane. Remaining: 5 files
(runtime.rs is wa-1m7nk; search_bridge.rs is CC-2; workflows/{mod,
runner,engine}.rs).

Verification:
- cargo check --lib (default features): pass
- scripts/check_no_runtime_regression.sh: ft-xbnl0.2.6 gate passes
- rustfmt --edition 2024 --check: clean

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -6,6 +6,23 @@
 //! - Testability: Mock implementations for unit tests
 //! - Consistency: Same data access patterns as robot mode
 //! - Decoupling: UI doesn't know about SQLite or storage internals
+//!
+//! # Cx-first migration (ft-xbnl0.2.2)
+//!
+//! This module is a sync → async bridge: the `QueryClient` trait methods are
+//! synchronous and own a dedicated `runtime_compat::Runtime` that executes the
+//! inner async blocks via `runtime.block_on(async { ... })`. There is no
+//! public `async fn` surface here to thread `&Cx` through explicitly — the
+//! Cx flows automatically inside each `block_on` because
+//! `runtime_compat::Runtime::block_on` registers a root Cx under the
+//! `asupersync-runtime` feature, and downstream async helpers
+//! (`runtime_compat::timeout`, `broadcast_recv`, etc.) acquire that Cx via
+//! `Cx::current()`.
+//!
+//! If the TUI ever exposes an `async fn` query entry point, that's when a
+//! `_cx` variant should be added, matching the pattern used in `retry.rs`,
+//! `caut.rs`, `session_retention.rs`, `telemetry.rs`, `watchdog.rs`,
+//! `restore_process.rs`, `survival.rs`, `events.rs`, and `wait.rs`.
 
 use std::path::PathBuf;
